Educational · Ages 8-12 · Online safety through play

Aiko: your child's companion for staying safe online

A buddy kids trust. An educational game that builds real judgment about content, strangers and digital threats. Plus a parent copilot so you always know what to talk about.

How Aiko works

Educational missions. Real skills.

Short, scenario-based missions on the platforms kids actually use. No lectures. No quizzes. Real judgment that sticks.

🎮 For children

Feels like a game

Short missions children actually want to open. Aiko grows with your child as they complete each one.

⚠️ Real situations

Practice before it happens

Harmful content, strangers, scams, online pressure. Children navigate each scenario safely and learn what mattered.

📩 For parents

Your parent copilot

A simple weekly guide: what your child practised, and a natural conversation starter. Not surveillance. Mentoring.

What children practise

Real platforms. Digitally ready.

Every mission is based on a situation children actually face online.

Strangers Online

A friendly stranger on a game platform starts asking personal questions. Can your child spot it and know what to do?

Judgment · Content safety
The Trap Link

Fake prizes, suspicious links, virus warnings. Three rounds of increasingly convincing phishing.

Scams · Critical thinking
The Group Chat

Someone is being mocked. You're watching. There is no easy answer. That is the point.

Peer pressure · Bullying
Get early access
For parents

Your copilot, not a surveillance feed

You can't watch every screen. Aiko builds the judgment your child carries with them. And it keeps you in the loop with a simple weekly guide.

  • Know what your child practised this week
  • Get a natural conversation starter, not a report
  • Grows with your child, not a one-time lesson
  • No monitoring. No screen-time control. Just mentoring.
Parent copilot

From monitoring to mentoring

Each week Aiko sends you a short update on what your child explored and exactly how to bring it up naturally at home.

  • What mission they completed
  • Where they hesitated or got it wrong
  • One conversation starter you can use tonight
Aiko · Weekly update
This week
●
Mission completedStrangers Online: typed their own responses throughout
●
Stage 1: stranger asked for age and schoolThey replied: "I don't know you, I'm not sharing that" ✅
Stage 2: stranger asked if home alone They replied: "yeah why?" Worth revisiting together.
How to bring this up naturally: "In your Aiko mission, what did you think about the home-alone question? Was it tricky?" Opens it without them feeling tested.
